title: Material World Model — Ontology
principle: Everything in chemistry is a state transition. A material is defined not only by its composition, but by (1) which STATE it is in, (2) what DRIVER moves it to another state, and (3) the RESULT of that transition. Materials exist inside a State Space; documents/patents/TGA/DSC/FTIR/experiments are EVIDENCE that updates the model — they are not the model.
note: This ontology defines the ENTITIES and RELATIONS of the world model. It comes BEFORE the State Schema (formal representation) and BEFORE the Transformation Engine (which acts on the model) — the same discipline as contract-before-engine. Documentation only.

entities.definition: A distinct condition a material can be in. The unit of the world model.
A directed change from one State to another under a Driver, via a Mechanism.
What moves a material between states.
The physical/chemical mechanism realising a transition.
A boundary between materials/phases/environment present in a state or created by a transition.
A measurable characteristic that changes across transitions (modulus, thermal conductivity, porosity, …).
A source observation (document, patent, TGA/DSC/FTIR/XRD/SEM, experiment) that UPDATES a State or Transition. Not the model — the update to it.
A physical/chemical/process/economic/regulatory boundary a state or transition must respect.
A validated, general transition rule — a Transition promoted to a Law through human-gated validation (FSCTM).

governance: Provenance required — no invented chemistry (Principle 7): every State and Transition traces to Evidence.
Predicted/inferred transitions are HYPOTHESES until validated (Principle 4).
A Transition becomes a Law only via human-gated validation — the FSCTM path (conflict -> breakdown -> recombination -> new law).
No known transition for a (state, driver) is a GAP that feeds Innovation Space / breakdown — reported, never invented.
